docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:7:# Feature: Prose Quality Support
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:8:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:9:**ID:** FEAT-027
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:10:**Status:** Not Started
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:11:**Priority:** P2
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:12:**Owner:** DDx Team
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:13:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:14:## Overview
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:15:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:16:DDx Prose Quality Support defines deterministic, explainable prose checks for
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:17:governing artifacts and user-facing docs. The goal is clearer, more specific,
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:18:voice-preserving writing in DDx materials, not AI-detection evasion.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:19:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:20:This feature introduces a default skill surface, a deterministic checker/rules
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:21:surface, and a docs command surface for reviewing changed prose. It produces
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:22:structural findings that can be reviewed, tracked, and later fed into review
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:23:workflow integration.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:24:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:25:## Problem Statement
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:26:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:27:**Current situation:** Governing artifacts and documentation can drift into
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:28:vague, generic, high-level prose that reads fine on a first pass but weakens
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:29:document-driven development. When prose is too generic, maintainers spend more
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:30:time inferring intent than executing it.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:31:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:32:**Pain points:**
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:33:- Technical docs hide concrete behavior behind broad claims and filler phrasing
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:34:- Planning docs lose actionable detail because the prose stops short of naming
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:35:  the actual decision, constraint, or boundary
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:36:- Public prose becomes polished but imprecise, which makes DDx harder to trust
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:37:  as a source of truth
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:38:- Reviewers have no deterministic prose-quality signal to point at when the
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:39:  problem is specific wording rather than missing requirements
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:40:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:41:**Desired outcome:** DDx can identify common AI-writing tropes and generic
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:42:prose patterns with deterministic rules, explain each finding, and suggest a
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:43:concrete edit without rewriting the author's voice away.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:44:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:45:## Users and Content Modes
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:46:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:47:### Primary Users
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:48:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:49:- DDx maintainers
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:50:- Project maintainers using DDx governing artifacts
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:51:- Agents writing or reviewing docs
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:52:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:53:### Content Modes
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:54:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:55:Prose quality checks apply to three modes:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:56:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:57:- **Technical** prose: feature specs, design docs, reference docs, and command
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:58:  descriptions
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:59:- **Planning** prose: beads, plans, roadmaps, and implementation notes
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:60:- **Public** prose: website copy, release notes, and externally visible docs
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:61:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:62:The rules may vary by mode, but they share the same deterministic output shape
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:63:and advisory default behavior.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:64:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:65:## Product Surfaces
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:66:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:67:### 1. Default Skill
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:68:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:69:DDx ships a default skill surface that can guide agents toward concise,
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:70:specific prose and surface the prose-quality workflow when appropriate. The
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:71:skill is advisory: it helps authors improve prose, but it does not change the
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:72:meaning of the authored artifact.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:73:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:74:### 2. Deterministic Checker and Rules
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:75:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:76:DDx defines prose-quality checks as deterministic rules over text. The checker
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:77:does not attempt to classify human vs. AI authorship and is not an AI detector.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:78:It evaluates observed text against named rules and explains each match.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:79:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:80:### 3. `ddx doc prose --changed`
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:81:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:82:DDx adds a docs surface for checking changed prose only. The command is meant
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:83:for pre-review and pre-merge use cases where maintainers want a focused,
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:84:diff-based advisory report instead of a full repository scan.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:85:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:86:### 4. Later Review Integration
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:87:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:88:The feature reserves room for later review integration so prose findings can be
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:89:surfaced in review workflows. This feature does not define that integration
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:90:boundary beyond naming it as the next consumer of the same deterministic
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:91:finding format.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:92:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:93:## Requirements
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:94:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:95:### Functional
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:96:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:97:1. **Deterministic rule evaluation** — prose checks must run as rule-based
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:98:   analysis over the selected text and return repeatable results for the same
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:99:   input.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:100:2. **Mode awareness** — the checker must support technical, planning, and
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:101:   public prose modes, with rule application appropriate to the selected mode.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:102:3. **Advisory default** — prose findings are advisory by default and do not
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:103:   block docs operations unless a later policy explicitly opts into blocking.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:104:4. **Structural findings** — each finding must include:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:105:   - file path
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:106:   - line or line range
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:107:   - rule id
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:108:   - severity
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:109:   - rationale
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:110:   - suggested edit
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:111:5. **Project vocabulary** — findings and suggestions must preserve project
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:112:   terminology when possible instead of rewriting terms into generic language.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:113:6. **Changed-only review surface** — `ddx doc prose --changed` evaluates only
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:114:   changed prose by default and reports findings for the touched lines.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:115:7. **Explainable output** — the checker must describe why a rule fired using
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:116:   concrete textual evidence from the input.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:117:8. **Later review compatibility** — the finding format must be stable enough to
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:118:   be consumed by future review integration without changing the core rule
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:119:   model.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:120:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:121:### Measurable Acceptance Criteria
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:122:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:123:The feature is considered successful when it can produce deterministic,
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:124:command-verifiable findings with the following structure for a changed prose
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:125:sample:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:126:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:127:- `file`: the path of the changed document
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:128:- `line` or `line_range`: the affected location
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:129:- `rule_id`: a stable deterministic identifier
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:130:- `severity`: advisory severity value
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:131:- `rationale`: a short explanation tied to the observed text
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:132:- `suggested_edit`: a concrete replacement or rewrite suggestion
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:133:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:134:The feature must also be able to flag generic prose patterns such as vague
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:135:claims, filler transitions, and uncoupled abstractions while preserving the
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:136:document's own vocabulary and intended voice.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:137:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:138:Findings must be structural rather than subjective. Each result must trace to a
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:139:specific file and line span, and the rationale must explain the triggered rule
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:140:in terms of observed text instead of a broad style judgment.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:141:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:142:## Non-Goals
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:143:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:144:- No AI detector
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:145:- No detector bypass
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:146:- No default blocking behavior
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:147:- No automatic rewriting that strips authorial voice
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:148:- No implementation of the checker in this feature
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:149:- No CLI flag design beyond naming the deterministic prose check surface
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:150:- No plugin asset additions
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:151:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:152:## Rule Model
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:153:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:154:The prose-quality checker should reason in terms of named rules, not opaque
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:155:scores. A rule may detect one or more common prose tropes, but each emitted
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:156:finding must remain explainable and reviewable on its own.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:157:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:158:### Example Rule Families
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:159:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:160:- Generic claim without specific subject or consequence
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:161:- Overly abstract sentence that omits the concrete artifact or action
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:162:- Repetition of empty emphasis phrases
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:163:- Passive or indirect phrasing where the responsible actor is known
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:164:- Voice drift that replaces project-specific terminology with generic wording
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:165:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:166:These families are examples of the target shape, not a final implementation
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:167:catalog.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:168:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:169:## Command and Skill Boundaries
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:170:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:171:- The default skill helps authors and agents notice prose issues during writing
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:172:  and review
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:173:- The deterministic checker owns the actual finding generation
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:174:- `ddx doc prose --changed` is the primary command surface for reviewing only
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:175:  changed prose
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:176:- Review integration is a later consumer of the same structured findings
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:177:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:178:## Out of Scope
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:179:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:180:- Detector scoring heuristics that try to infer authorship
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:181:- Content transformation that rewrites style by default
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:182:- Blocking docs operations by default
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:183:- Choosing the final low-level implementation boundary beyond naming
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:184:  deterministic prose checks
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md:185:- CLI flags or plugin assets beyond the prose review surface

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:34:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:35:DDx should ship a pluggable runner wrapper whose canonical first runner

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:37:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:38:- The deterministic prose logic lives in DDx, not in Vale.

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:41:  runner based on configuration, and degrade gracefully when external
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:42:  tooling is missing.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:43:

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:47:- the first executable surface does not depend on a third-party install
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:48:- optional tooling can be added later without changing the finding

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:52:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:53:### Default path
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:54:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:55:The default path is the embedded checker. It runs in-process and does not
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:56:require Vale or any other external binary.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:57:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:58:### Optional runner path
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:59:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:60:The wrapper may delegate to Vale when a project explicitly selects that
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:61:runner. That path is compatibility-oriented and must produce the same
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:62:finding schema as the embedded checker.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:63:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:64:### Missing-tool behavior
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:65:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:66:If the selected optional runner is unavailable, DDx must not turn the
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:67:prose check into a hard failure by default.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:68:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:69:- In `policy: advisory`, DDx falls back to the embedded checker when it
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:70:  can, or reports a single advisory diagnostic that the optional runner
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:71:  was unavailable.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:72:- In `policy: blocking`, DDx still prefers to run the embedded checker
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:73:  so the user gets concrete findings; an unavailable optional runner is
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:74:  reported as an execution diagnostic, not as a prose finding.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:75:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:76:The important rule is that missing optional tooling never erases the
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:77:document analysis path. It only changes whether DDx can use the selected
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:78:runner implementation.

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:186:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:187:Findings must be structural and machine-readable. The canonical fields
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:188:are:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:189:

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:192:- `rule_id`
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:193:- `severity`
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:194:- `rationale`
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:195:- `suggested_edit`
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:196:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:197:Each finding therefore carries file, line, and rule identifiers together
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:198:with an explanation and a concrete edit suggestion.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:199:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:200:Implementation may add helper fields such as `mode`, `snippet`, or
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:201:`runner`, but these core fields must remain stable.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:202:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:203:Rules for each field:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:204:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:205:- `file` is the relative path of the affected document.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:206:- `line` or `line_range` identifies the touched text span.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:207:- `rule_id` is a stable deterministic identifier, not a prose label.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:208:- `severity` reflects the configured policy and the rule’s native

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:210:- `rationale` must explain why the rule fired using observed text.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:211:- `suggested_edit` must propose a concrete rewrite, replacement, or
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:212:  deletion.
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:213:
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:214:The TD-027-style principle applies here too: the output must be specific
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:215:enough that a later review consumer can reuse it without changing the
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md:216:rule model.

     3	  id: TD-036
     4	  depends_on:
     5	    - FEAT-027
     6	  status: draft
     7	---
     8	# Technical Design: Prose Quality Pipeline
     9	
    10	## Status
    11	
    12	Draft. This TD closes the implementation-boundary gap left open by
    13	FEAT-027 by deciding how DDx evaluates prose deterministically, how the
    14	default plugin packages the assets, and how the first command surface
    15	behaves when optional tooling is unavailable.
    16	
    17	## Why This TD Exists
    18	
    19	FEAT-027 defines the product problem and the required output shape for
    20	deterministic prose checks, but it intentionally stops short of naming
    21	the execution boundary. That leaves three open questions for the first
    22	implementation bead:
    23	
    24	- whether DDx should shell out to Vale
    25	- whether DDx should embed a small checker
    26	- whether DDx should wrap a pluggable runner and treat external tooling
    27	  as optional
    28	
    29	This TD answers the checker boundary question so the implementation
    30	beads can start from a stable contract instead of inventing their own
    31	architecture.
    32	
    33	## Decision
    34	
    35	DDx should ship a pluggable runner wrapper whose canonical first runner
    36	is an embedded checker.
    37	
    38	- The deterministic prose logic lives in DDx, not in Vale.
    39	- Vale is an optional compatibility runner, not the required core.
    40	- The wrapper is what lets DDx keep the first surface advisory, select a
    41	  runner based on configuration, and degrade gracefully when external
    42	  tooling is missing.
    43	
    44	That boundary keeps the product contract stable:
    45	
    46	- the checker is deterministic and explainable
    47	- the first executable surface does not depend on a third-party install
    48	- optional tooling can be added later without changing the finding
    49	  schema
    50	
    51	## Runtime Behavior
    52	
    53	### Default path
    54	
    55	The default path is the embedded checker. It runs in-process and does not
    56	require Vale or any other external binary.
    57	
    58	### Optional runner path
    59	
    60	The wrapper may delegate to Vale when a project explicitly selects that
    61	runner. That path is compatibility-oriented and must produce the same
    62	finding schema as the embedded checker.
    63	
    64	### Missing-tool behavior
    65	
    66	If the selected optional runner is unavailable, DDx must not turn the
    67	prose check into a hard failure by default.
    68	
    69	- In `policy: advisory`, DDx falls back to the embedded checker when it
    70	  can, or reports a single advisory diagnostic that the optional runner
    71	  was unavailable.
    72	- In `policy: blocking`, DDx still prefers to run the embedded checker
    73	  so the user gets concrete findings; an unavailable optional runner is
    74	  reported as an execution diagnostic, not as a prose finding.
    75	
    76	The important rule is that missing optional tooling never erases the
    77	document analysis path. It only changes whether DDx can use the selected
    78	runner implementation.
    79	
    80	## Default Plugin Asset Layout
    81	
    82	The prose-quality assets belong in the default DDx plugin, not in a
    83	project-specific check scaffold.
    84	
    85	Proposed source layout:
    86	
    87	- `library/checks/prose-quality/check.yaml`
    88	- `library/checks/prose-quality/rules/`
    89	- `library/checks/prose-quality/vocabulary/`
    90	- `library/checks/prose-quality/fixtures/`
    91	
    92	Installed layout:
    93	
    94	- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/checks/prose-quality/check.yaml`
    95	- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/checks/prose-quality/rules/`
    96	- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/checks/prose-quality/vocabulary/`
    97	- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/checks/prose-quality/fixtures/`
    98	
    99	Layout rules:
   100	
   101	- `rules/` stores named rule definitions grouped by mode.
   102	- `vocabulary/` stores project terminology that the checker should
   103	  preserve or prefer.
   104	- `fixtures/` stores golden inputs and expected findings for regression
   105	  tests.
   106	- `check.yaml` wires the default command invocation and the runner
   107	  selection defaults.
   108	
   109	The layout is intentionally check-shaped rather than skill-shaped. The
   110	skill can point authors at the workflow, but the asset tree owns the
   111	deterministic rule definitions.
   112	
   113	## Config Schema Sketch
   114	
   115	The config schema needs to expose the policy knobs without making the
   116	first release overfit to one runner.
   117	
   118	```yaml
   119	prose:
   120	  mode: technical | planning | public
   121	  severity: advisory | warning | blocking
   122	  policy: advisory | blocking
   123	  runner: embedded | vale | auto
   124	  includes:
   125	    - docs/helix/**
   126	  excludes:
   127	    - "**/*.generated.md"
   128	  vocabulary:
   129	    accept:
   130	      - DDx
   131	      - bead
   132	      - execution
   133	    reject:
   134	      - system
   135	      - solution
   136	      - seamless
   137	```
   138	
   139	Semantics:
   140	
   141	- `mode` selects the rule pack.
   142	- `severity` is the severity attached to emitted findings.
   143	- `policy` controls whether findings are advisory by default or can be
   144	  elevated to blocking later.
   145	- `runner` selects the implementation boundary.
   146	- `includes` and `excludes` define the text selection scope.
   147	- `vocabulary.accept` preserves project terms and domain terms.
   148	- `vocabulary.reject` names generic substitutes the checker should flag
   149	  when they replace project vocabulary.
   150	
   151	`policy: advisory` is the default. That is the product-level default
   152	required by FEAT-027 and the default the first executable surface must
   153	honor.
   154	
   155	## CLI Shape
   156	
   157	The CLI surface is intentionally small.
   158	
   159	### Primary command
   160	
   161	`ddx doc prose --changed`
   162	
   163	This is the first supported surface. It checks changed prose only and is
   164	the default entry point for pre-review and pre-merge usage.
   165	
   166	### Future direct-path command
   167	
   168	`ddx doc prose <paths>`
   169	
   170	This is the future explicit-path form. It should accept one or more
   171	paths, reuse the same engine, and allow a caller to target a fixed set of
   172	documents without relying on the diff.
   173	
   174	### Shared behavior
   175	
   176	Both forms must:
   177	
   178	- load the same rule pack and vocabulary assets
   179	- emit the same finding schema
   180	- respect the same advisory/blocking policy
   181	- preserve the same mode-specific rule selection
   182	
   183	The only difference is how the input set is selected.
   184	
   185	## Finding Schema
   186	
   187	Findings must be structural and machine-readable. The canonical fields
   188	are:
   189	
   190	- `file`
   191	- `line` or `line_range`
   192	- `rule_id`
   193	- `severity`
   194	- `rationale`
   195	- `suggested_edit`
   196	
   197	Each finding therefore carries file, line, and rule identifiers together
   198	with an explanation and a concrete edit suggestion.
   199	
   200	Implementation may add helper fields such as `mode`, `snippet`, or
   201	`runner`, but these core fields must remain stable.
   202	
   203	Rules for each field:
   204	
   205	- `file` is the relative path of the affected document.
   206	- `line` or `line_range` identifies the touched text span.
   207	- `rule_id` is a stable deterministic identifier, not a prose label.
   208	- `severity` reflects the configured policy and the rule’s native
   209	  impact.
   210	- `rationale` must explain why the rule fired using observed text.
   211	- `suggested_edit` must propose a concrete rewrite, replacement, or
   212	  deletion.
   213	
   214	The TD-027-style principle applies here too: the output must be specific
   215	enough that a later review consumer can reuse it without changing the
   216	rule model.
   217	
   218	## Fixture And Golden Test Plan
   219	
   220	The first implementation bead should be guided by fixture-driven golden

220	The first implementation bead should be guided by fixture-driven golden
   221	tests rather than ad hoc assertions.
   222	
   223	Recommended fixture set:
   224	
   225	- one technical sample with vague claims and uncoupled abstractions
   226	- one planning sample with generic roadmap language
   227	- one public sample with voice drift and filler phrases
   228	- one sample with accepted project vocabulary that must be preserved
   229	- one sample with an unavailable optional runner
   230	
   231	Recommended test shape:
   232	
   233	- `TestProseCheckerChangedMode`
   234	- `TestProseCheckerPathMode`
   235	- `TestProseCheckerFindingSchema`
   236	- `TestProseCheckerVocabularyPreservation`
   237	- `TestProseCheckerMissingRunnerFallsBackOrReportsAdvisory`
   238	
   239	Golden assertions should lock down:
   240	
   241	- the chosen `rule_id`
   242	- the affected line span
   243	- the advisory default behavior
   244	- the suggested edit text
   245	- the missing-tool diagnostic text
   246	
   247	The fixtures should be stable text files and JSON expectations so that a
   248	future runner swap does not invalidate the acceptance corpus.
   249	
   250	## Sequencing
   251	
   252	The rollout should be staged in this order:
   253	
   254	1. skill and rule assets
   255	2. deterministic `ddx doc prose --changed`
   256	3. direct-path `ddx doc prose <paths>`
   257	4. bead review integration later, after the core command and result
   258	   schema are stable
   259	
   260	That sequencing keeps the first executable surface advisory and
   261	deterministic before any review workflow starts consuming the findings.
   262	
   263	## Non-Scope
   264	
   265	- No CLI implementation in this TD
   266	- No rule file content in this TD
   267	- No Vale packaging requirement
   268	- No bead review wiring in this TD
   269	- No automatic prose rewriting

   230	
   231	- one technical sample with vague claims and uncoupled abstractions
   232	- one planning sample with generic roadmap language
   233	- one public sample with voice drift and filler phrases
   234	- one sample with accepted project vocabulary that must be preserved
   235	- one sample with an unavailable optional runner
   236	
   237	Recommended test shape:
   238	
   239	- `TestProseCheckerChangedMode`
   240	- `TestProseCheckerPathMode`
   241	- `TestProseCheckerFindingSchema`
   242	- `TestProseCheckerVocabularyPreservation`
   243	- `TestProseCheckerMissingRunnerFallsBackOrReportsAdvisory`
   244	
   245	Golden assertions should lock down:
   246	
   247	- the chosen `rule_id`
   248	- the affected line span
   249	- the advisory default behavior
   250	- the suggested edit text
   251	- the missing-tool diagnostic text
   252	
   253	The fixtures should be stable text files and JSON expectations so that a
   254	future runner swap does not invalidate the acceptance corpus.
   255	
   256	## Sequencing
   257	
   258	The rollout should be staged in this order:
   259	
   260	1. skill and rule assets
   261	2. deterministic `ddx doc prose --changed`
   262	3. direct-path `ddx doc prose <paths>`
   263	4. bead review integration later, after the core command and result
   264	   schema are stable
   265	
   266	That sequencing keeps the first executable surface advisory and
   267	deterministic before any review workflow starts consuming the findings.
